alerts.title: Iran's uranium enrichment remains at critical 60% U-235; breakout window 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran continues enrichment at 60% U-235 with estimated 1-2 week breakout timeframe. US-Iran MoU text leaked by Middle East Spectator (~31K Telegram views) reveals zero nuclear commitments from Iran, undermining deal credibility and escalating risk of rapid weaponization if diplomatic collapse occurs (global_metrics, telegram).

alerts.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon intensify despite Trump rebuke; Iran vows retaliation
alerts.summary: Israeli Air Force conducted additional drone strikes in southern Lebanon killing at least 3 civilians per NBC News and Reuters (2026-06-17). Iran's senior lawmakers publicly declared 'strong response coming' after Beirut strike. Trump criticized Netanyahu as having 'no fucking judgment' but operations persist, escalating tit-for-tat cycle amid fragile MoU (incidents, telegram).

alerts.title: Pentagon confirms AI-assisted targeting system used against Iran in 'Epic Fury' operation
alerts.summary: Rybar Telegram channel (~65K views, 2026-06-17) reports Pentagon officially acknowledged deployment of Grok Gov Model AI from Elon Musk's xAI in Maven Smart System operations against Iran, enabling ~2000 missile strikes on 2000 targets within 96 hours. Represents significant escalation in AI-driven warfare capability and scale of kinetic operations (telegram).

alerts.title: Iran begins oil exports from Strait of Hormuz after months, testing deal implementation
alerts.summary: Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty reports Iran's first oil shipments in months now departing Strait of Hormuz (2026-06-17 incidents feed), signaling partial economic confidence despite deal tensions. Follows Trump's public statement that he'll 'resume bombing if Iran acts up,' creating fragile equilibrium where both sides probe for compliance violations (incidents, global_metrics).

alerts.title: Three US carrier strike groups fully deployed across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Mediterranean
alerts.summary: Asset feeds confirm USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) operationally deployed and positioned to respond to Iran escalation. Alongside Red Sea destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon), amphibious assets (Bataan), and submarine (Florida in Persian Gulf), creates overwhelming forward presence matching threat level 9 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION per global_metrics, 2026-06-17).
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf, Mediterranean

analysis_note: US-Iran MoU remains critically fragile: nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks amid zero Iranian commitments; Israeli-Lebanese escalation continues despite Trump pressure; Pentagon AI-assisted targeting enables unprecedented strike scale; forward-deployed US carrier task forces and allied proxies (Houthis, Hezbollah) remain at elevated alert—next 72 hours will determine whether deal survives or collapse triggers full-scale regional conflict.
